back_translation: For the choirmaster. According to Jeduthun. A psalm of David.
For God alone my soul waits in silence; from Him comes my salvation.
He alone is my rock and my salvation, my fortress; I shall not be greatly shaken.
How long will you assault a man? Would all of you batter him down, like a leaning wall, a tottering fence?
They plan to topple him from his high position; they delight in lies; with their mouths they bless, but inwardly they curse. Selah
For God alone, O my soul, wait in silence, for my hope comes from Him.
He alone is my rock and my salvation, my fortress; I shall not be shaken.
My salvation and my honor rest on God; the rock of my strength, my refuge, is in God.
Trust in Him at all times, O people; pour out your hearts before Him; God is a refuge for us. Selah
Surely the common man is but a breath, the man of rank a delusion; weighed on the scales, they are lighter than a breath together.
Do not trust in extortion, and do not put vain hope in robbery; though riches increase, do not set your heart on them.
Once God has spoken; twice I have heard this: that power belongs to God,
and to You, O Lord, belongs steadfast love; for You render to each according to his work.
